plan.title: RFC: Agentic Prioritization & Coordination Model for Deft Directive

narratives.Description: RFC: Agentic Prioritization & Coordination Model for Deft Directive
narratives.Overview:
# RFC: Agentic Prioritization & Coordination Model for Deft Directive

**Supersedes**: #987
**Status**: Draft for Discussion (rev 13)
**Date**: 2026-06-02

_Rev 11 folded in research-informed changes (review/autonomy, outcome loop, risk-tiered gates, cost overlay, drift, receipts). Rev 12 added the **cost-capture plan** (start collecting actuals now). Rev 13 grounds cost capture on the **Warp Enterprise Analytics API** (per-message `credit_charged` + `git_context` over both interactive and cloud runs), adds a `credits` cost field + `task capacity:cost-sync`, and makes the overlay two-tier (enterprise-grounded vs estimate-only); warpdotdev/warp#12075 is narrowed to sub-enterprise access + raw token/$ exposure. Open Questions v2 tracks loosened items; full revision history is in the GitHub edit log._

## Context & Problem Statement
#987 identified that there is no first-class, sustainable way to express ordering or priority *between* separate vBRIEFs for swarm allocation and ROADMAP rendering. Today this is emergent: alphabetical filename order, selective promotion to `active/`, and operator judgment at allocation time. These are workable at current scale but fragile as agentic velocity rises, and per-item "urgent" work systematically displaces important-but-non-urgent work, accelerating debt.

This RFC proposes a durable model that (a) answers #987's concrete ordering gap, and (b) adds capacity protection and judgment gates so high-velocity agentic work doesn't quietly starve debt paydown or bypass human judgment on sensitive changes. Research note: the dominant industry failure mode is the *operating model*, not tooling (2025 DORA: throughput rises while stability slips); the bottleneck has shifted from execution to **human judgment/review capacity**. This RFC is an operating-model artifact and rev 11+ make the outflow side (review capacity, outcome quality, cost) first-class, not just the inflow side.

## Definitions of Debt Types
- **Technical Debt** ‚Äî suboptimal code/architecture that raises the cost/risk of future change.
- **Cognitive Debt** ‚Äî erosion of shared human understanding when the rate of (often agent-produced) change outpaces the team's ability to maintain a coherent mental model.
- **Agentic Debt (Orchestration Debt)** ‚Äî brittle prompts/orchestration, unowned eval harnesses, tool-schema drift, context-management issues, and the growing agent-infrastructure surface that must be governed.

These are distinct. v1 buckets cognitive + agentic together; **context/doc drift** is the emerging measurable signal that could graduate cognitive debt to its own bucket (see Deferred to v2 + Open Questions v2).

## Goals
- Define a sustainable model for priority, ordering, and coordination between vBRIEFs.
- Make human judgment explicit and first-class where it adds value; keep it minimal on small projects.
- Treat human-clearance capacity, outcome quality, and cost as first-class, not just execution effort.
- Support brownfield and greenfield adoption without disruptive migrations.
- Work across scales (solo ‚Üí multi-team).
- Prefer conventions/skills/metadata over vBRIEF schema changes.

## Non-Goals
- A universal numeric priority field every vBRIEF must maintain.
- Heavy ceremony on small projects.
- Fully automating prioritization (or human judgment) away.
- Replacing lightweight mechanisms that still work.
- A full agent control-plane (identity/SIEM/observability beyond decisions); deft's existing gate stack owns that.

## Guiding Principles
- **Reuse before invent.** Express new behavior through existing machinery (`plan.policy`, the `triageAutoClassify` match engine, `wipCap`, `triageRankingLabels`, the `verify:*` deterministic-gate pattern, the policy audit-row pattern, `migrate:vbrief`, the review-cycle skill, `deft-directive-cost`).
- **vBRIEF-canonical, SCM-agnostic.** The vBRIEF is the source of truth; SCM labels are an optional one-way mirror. Everything reads filesystem-truth and runs offline.
- **Deterministic enforcement.** Per the Rule Authority [AXIOM], anything load-bearing graduates from prose to a script gate wired into `task check` / hooks ‚Äî or it erodes (cf. #798 / #954 / #1369).
- **Close the loop.** Inflow (what/how-much/order) is governed alongside outflow (review capacity, outcome quality, cost, earned autonomy).
- **Human judgment scales down gracefully**; auditable and explainable by default.

## Proposed Direction
Three orthogonal layers, all declared at the PROJECT-DEFINITION level:

1. **Capacity Allocation** ‚Äî *how much* effort per category (protected buckets), measured over the completion flow + outcome & cost overlays.
2. **Judgment Gates** ‚Äî *what must be human-cleared* before promotion/dispatch (risk-tiered).
3. **Selection Ordering** ‚Äî *which ready item next* (the #987 answer + continuation precedence).

Cross-cutting: **Session-Start Nudge Budgeting** keeps surfaces quiet; **Review, Autonomy & Human-Clearance Capacity** governs the outflow side; **Brownfield Backfill** makes adoption painless.

### Layer 1 ‚Äî Capacity Allocation
Protected effort buckets bias what gets promoted/worked so debt categories aren't starved by urgent work. **Capacity measures effort distribution, not delivered value** ‚Äî it tracks where effort went. Outcome/value and cost are measured separately (overlays) so they aren't conflated.

**Default buckets (v1):**

```json
"buckets": [
  { "id": "new-capability", "target": 0.50 },
  { "id": "technical-debt", "target": 0.20 },
  { "id": "agentic-debt",   "target": 0.15 },
  { "id": "urgent",         "target": 0.15 }
]
```

**Denominator (v1: vBRIEF count, filesystem-truth).** Capacity is measured as a count of scope vBRIEFs ‚Äî the same unit `wipCap` already counts. `unit: "weighted"` (size‚Üíweight) and `unit: "cost"` (credit/$ per vBRIEF) are defined-but-deferred; v1 ships `vbrief-count` with cost surfaced as a reporting overlay (see Cost capture).

**`kind`-aware counting (epics).** Only `kind: "story"` is executable; epics/phases are containers:
- `kind == "story"` ‚Üí count its weight.
- `kind in {epic, phase}` **with** children ‚Üí count **0**; children counted directly.
- `kind in {epic, phase}` **without** children (undecomposed) ‚Üí count `plan.metadata.estimatedChildren` (`defaultEpicEstimate`, default 3, when unset).

The epic estimate only affects the **forward** view; backward adherence counts real completed stories. Children inherit `capacityBucket` from the parent epic (via `planRef`). The same rule feeds `wipCap`.

**`estimatedChildren` lifecycle.** In-flight grooming metadata: set at `scope:promote`; refinement may update; **retired at `scope:decompose`**. Field presence is the groomed signal ‚Äî *absent* counts via `defaultEpicEstimate` AND fires a "needs estimation" nudge; *present* goes stale only on time (`epicStalenessDays`, default 14).

**Stranded slices (partially-completed epics).** A feature whose first slice landed but whose mates never did is debt. The completed slice **keeps its bucket** (freeze holds). The debt is **forward-recognized**: a partially-completed epic dormant > `epicStrandedDays` (default 30) is flagged with a **trichotomy** ‚Äî finish / cancel & remove (file removal as tech-debt) / accept & file a tech-debt vBRIEF.

**Accounting window (required).** *Forward* (in-flight `pending/`+`active/` vs target ‚Üí biases promotion) and *backward* (completed over a trailing window vs target ‚Üí the report). `window: { kind: "trailing-days" | "trailing-completed", value: N }` (default `trailing-days: 30`).

**Enforcement is flow-based, not snapshot-based (small-N reality).**
- `wipCap` governs **concurrency**; buckets govern **longitudinal mix** over the completion stream. Different axes.
- Targets are enforced against **completed work over the trailing window**, not the in-flight snapshot.
- Selection is **deficit-biased** at `scope:promote` and cohort-fill (`deficit = target ‚àí actual`), applied at cohort assembly so lumps self-correct.
- **`minSampleSize` guard** (default 20): below this many **classified** completions (carrying `capacityBucket` + `completedAt`), capacity is advisory and selection falls back to Layer-3 ordering + `wipCap`. Counting *classified* completions prevents pre-adoption history from falsely activating capacity (see Brownfield Backfill).

**Outcome signal (closing the loop).** Capacity is an *input* metric; pair it with an *outcome* metric so a bucket can't be "busy but failing" invisibly. A per-bucket **outcome health overlay** (does NOT change the allocation metric): the **revert/rework rate** ‚Äî git reverts plus follow-up fix vBRIEFs that `reference` a completed vBRIEF ‚Äî computed filesystem-truth over the same window. Optionally integrate an external code-health/coverage signal where available. Surfaced in `capacity:show`; sustained high rework is a Tier-2 nudge.

**Where running totals live (derived, not stored).** Tallies computed on demand from the lifecycle folders: backward from `vbrief/completed/` (frozen `capacityBucket` + `completedAt`), forward from `pending/`+`active/`. Deficit computed at decision time. Optional rebuildable cache in `vbrief/.eval/` only; never authoritative.

**Cost capture (rev 11 overlay; rev 12 capture plan; rev 13 Analytics-API grounding).** Agentic work has real, variable credit/$ cost; a count-based denominator can hide that one bucket is many times the spend of another. `capacity:show` surfaces a per-bucket **cost** column. Capture mechanics:
- **`plan.metadata.cost = { credits, tokens, usd, source, partial, asOf }`** is stamped on completed vBRIEFs whenever a figure is available ‚Äî additive metadata that defaults absent, so projects **start collecting actuals now** and history accrues (you cannot backfill spend from git, so starting early matters ‚Äî same logic as `completedAt`). `credits` is the native Warp unit; `usd` is derived via a configured credit‚Üí$ rate; `tokens` is populated only when a token-level source is available (the Warp Analytics API reports credits, not tokens).
- **Capture from telemetry, never agent self-report** (models can't accurately report their own usage). Primary source: the **Warp Enterprise Analytics API** (`/enterprises/analytics/events` + `/users`), which exposes per-message `credit_charged` over **both `local` interactive and `cloud` runs** ‚Äî directly covering the surface that was previously a gap. Supplementary token-level sources where present: the model API `usage` field; CLI-harness OTEL/usage logs (Claude Code / Codex); the `oz` CLI / Oz API per-run usage.
- **Attribution ‚Äî directive already owns the join key.** Analytics `events` rows carry `git_context.branch` + `head` and `conversation_id`; the swarm worktree flow assigns a known branch per story, so cost maps to a vBRIEF by branch/commit (fallback: `conversation_id` recorded at `scope:complete`). The `vBRIEF:` trailer / cohort manifest still splits a batched session across its stories; include review-cycle iterations, retries, and failed attempts (rework cost is signal, not noise).
- **Post-hoc sync (`task capacity:cost-sync`).** Analytics data lands minutes after a run (no inline figure), so a batch job queries `events` over the completion window, groups `credit_charged` by branch / `conversation_id`, and writes `cost` onto the matching completed vBRIEFs. Idempotent + re-runnable (same shape as `verify:*` / backfill). The same rows' `was_edited_by_user` / `code_changes` feed the outcome/rework overlay.
- **Provenance:** `source` (`warp-analytics-api` | `api-usage` | `harness-otel` | `platform` | `estimated`) + a `partial` flag (true when reviewer/cloud cost isn't captured), mirroring `capacityBucketSource`, so later analysis knows reliability.
- **Estimate-vs-actual calibration:** `deft-directive-cost` produces a pre-build estimate; these post-build actuals give estimate-vs-actual variance, which calibrates future estimates and is itself an outcome/health signal (a bucket whose actuals routinely exceed estimate is flagged).
- **Coverage is two-tier.** *Enterprise teams:* grounded for interactive + cloud via the Analytics API (admin-only; requires Admin Panel ‚Üí Privacy *Enterprise Usage Reporting*). *Below enterprise (Pro/individual/non-admin):* no programmatic surface ‚Äî cost stays **estimate-only** (`deft-directive-cost`) until per-task usage is exposed. Remaining upstream asks (sub-enterprise access; raw token/$ vs credits) tracked at **warpdotdev/warp#12075**. Promoting cost to a capacity `unit` is deferred (Open Questions v2).

**Bucket assignment (vBRIEF-canonical).** `plan.metadata.capacityBucket` (authoritative, SCM-neutral) + `plan.metadata.capacityBucketSource` (`match`|`agent`|`human`). Bucket `match` blocks only *propose*; human-confirmed wins. Unassigned ‚Üí `defaultBucket`. **Re-bucketing** allowed in-flight, frozen at completion.

**`wipCap` reconciliation.** Hard ceiling; buckets are soft targets that bias selection. `advise` (default) deprioritizes over-target buckets + relief hint; `block` refuses over-target promotion with `--force`. A gate-blocked story still consumes its bucket's capacity (reserved); `capacity:show` shows a per-bucket `blocked` sub-count. **wipCap should be set relative to human-clearance throughput, not just machine concurrency** (see Review & Autonomy).

**Schema:**

```json
{ "plan": { "policy": { "capacityAllocation": {
  "unit": "vbrief-count",
  "window": { "kind": "trailing-days", "value": 30 },
  "enforcement": "advise",
  "minSampleSize": 20,
  "defaultBucket": "new-capability",
  "defaultEpicEstimate": 3,
  "epicStalenessDays": 14,
  "epicStrandedDays": 30,
  "finishBeforeStart": false,
  "buckets": [
    { "id": "new-capability", "target": 0.50, "match": { "labels": { "any-of": ["enhancement","feature"] } } },
    { "id": "technical-debt", "target": 0.20, "match": { "labels": { "any-of": ["tech-debt","refactor"] } } },
    { "id": "agentic-debt",   "target": 0.15, "match": { "labels": { "any-of": ["skills","orchestration","eval"] } } },
    { "id": "urgent",         "target": 0.15, "match": { "labels": { "any-of": ["urgent","blocks-release-tag"] } } }
  ]
} } } }
```

Validator: `sum(target) == 1.0` (¬±epsilon); unique ids; window present; `minSampleSize ‚â§ window.value` when `kind = "trailing-completed"`.

**Surfaces:** `task capacity:show` (target vs actual both views; per-bucket `blocked` sub-count, **outcome/rework rate**, **cost**; active/advisory mode line; full ranked nudge list; filesystem-truth, offline); a budgeted one-liner in `task triage:welcome`; `task verify:capacity` (three-state, advisory by default).

### Layer 2 ‚Äî Judgment Gates (risk-tiered)
A judgment gate marks work that must be human-cleared before promotion to `active/` or dispatch. Orthogonal to priority.

**Reuse, don't reinvent.** Gates use the existing `triageAutoClassify` match DSL (`labels`, `body-text`, `state`, `age-days`) + a new `paths{any-of}` (glob) predicate, in `plan.policy.judgmentGates[]`.

```json
{
  "id": "secrets-and-credentials",
  "class": "mechanical",
  "match": {
    "labels":    { "any-of": ["area:secrets","security"] },
    "body-text": { "any-of": ["secret","credential","api key","token"] },
    "paths":     { "any-of": ["secrets/**","**/*.env","**/credentials*"] }
  },
  "tier": "block",
  "requiredHumanReviewers": 2,
  "reason": "touches secrets / credential handling -- requires human sign-off"
}
```

**Risk tiers, not binary.** Each gate carries a **tier** mapping to required human oversight ON TOP of automated multi-LLM review:
- `tier: "auto"` ‚Üí automated multi-LLM review clearance suffices (0 human reviewers); annotate-only.
- `tier: "review"` ‚Üí 1 human reviewer/clearance required.
- `tier: "block"` ‚Üí 2-person review (+ threat-model for the highest-risk gates); hard-fail until cleared.

`requiredHumanReviewers` makes this explicit. Risk derives from surface area, sensitivity, blast radius, novelty; default-on safety gates default to `block`. Tiers compose with the autonomy dial (Review & Autonomy).

**Two gate classes.** `mechanical` (labels/body-text/paths): `secrets-and-credentials`, `production-infrastructure`, `agents-md-and-skills`, `installer-and-bootstrap`, `data-access-boundaries`, `financial-or-billing`, `high-risk-external-integrations`. `declared` (subjective, explicit tag): `core-orchestration`, `large-architectural-changes`, `compliance-and-audit`, `evaluation-and-safety`.

**`declared`-gate detection.** Tagged + no clearance ‚Üí deterministic, honors tier. Untagged ‚Üí advisory annotation only. Optional `paths` backstop. Mechanical gates fail-closed on detection; declared gates fail-open on omission.

**Default-on universal safety gates.** Hardcoded, default-on (`secrets-and-credentials`, `production-infrastructure`, `agents-md-and-skills`, `installer-and-bootstrap`); disabled only by explicit audited `judgmentGatesDisabled` or lowering the tier.

**Default `match` globs ("both, layered").** Framework ships portable Tier-A globs on universals; deft's own globs live in deft's PROJECT-DEFINITION. Consumers extend via additive union by `id`.

**SCM-agnostic tags.** `declared` tags live in `plan.metadata.judgmentGateTags[]` (filesystem-truth, offline). `match.labels` is GitHub-only behind `scripts/scm.py` (`verify:scm-boundary`, #1145).

**Two enforcement points.** (1) Promotion/allocation time ‚Äî declared signals + `paths` vs `plan.metadata.swarm.file_scope[]`. (2) PR time (omission-proof) ‚Äî match the actual diff; an uncleared fired gate fails the PR check. Clearance binds to a `cleared_scope` fingerprint; scope creep re-triggers.

**Deterministic surface.** `scripts/verify_judgment_gates.py` ‚Üí `task verify:judgment-gates`, three-state exit, wired into `task check` + `.githooks/pre-push` + a `judgment-gate` GH Actions workflow. Bypass `DEFT_ALLOW_UNGATED=1`. New `pathspec` matcher.

**Integration with the gate stack (#1378 / #1387).** `## Allocation context` gains `gate_clearances[]` (`{ gate_id, vbrief_path, cleared_by, rationale, cleared_at, cleared_scope }`). `task verify:story-ready` (Gate 0) fails on an uncleared active `block` gate. Headless `swarm:launch` (#1387): a gated story rides the consent token only if its clearance is pre-recorded.

**Multi-vBRIEF PR attribution.** Per-story checkpoint commits carry a `vBRIEF:` trailer ‚Üí map commits to stories ‚Üí partition diff ‚Üí check per story. Path outside owning `file_scope[]` = scope creep; unattributable path hitting a `block` gate fails closed; runs pre-merge on unsquashed commits. v1 ships block-gated stories solo.

**Audit + precedence.** Clearances append to the durable audit log (see Receipts & Audit). First-match-wins (universals first); clearance per-gate-id.

### Layer 3 ‚Äî Selection Ordering (the #987 answer + continuation precedence)
Capacity decides *how much*; Layer 3 decides *which ready item next*. Selection ‚Äî in `task triage:queue` and swarm Phase 0 cohort-fill ‚Äî is a lexicographic sort:

```
( urgent/blocking down, continuation down, bucket-deficit down, intra-bucket rank down, date up )
```

- **urgent/blocking** (highest) ‚Äî `triageRankingLabels`; preempts everything incl. continuation.
- **continuation** ‚Äî a story whose `planRef` parent epic has already *started* (‚â•1 child completed or sibling active). Outranks net-new single-issue work ("stop starting, start finishing"); oldest-started epic first; overrides the bucket deficit short-term by design.
- **bucket-deficit** ‚Äî among net-new work, most-under-target bucket vs the trailing-window mix.
- **intra-bucket rank, then date** ‚Äî `plan.metadata.rank` (vBRIEF-intrinsic mirror of `triageRankingLabels`), then creation date.

**Spec-readiness eligibility.** Only work that meets the swarm-readiness/spec-quality bar is promotable/selectable ‚Äî under-specified work is refused with a pointer to `scope:decompose` / refinement ("bad spec = √ó10 waste"); reuse the `task swarm:readiness` contract.

**Escape valve:** continuation precedence is safe because **cancel** exists. Optional hard variant `finishBeforeStart: true`: at/near `wipCap`, only continuation work is promotable.

Wiring `plan.metadata.rank` is still the smallest independently-shippable slice and closes #987.

## Review, Autonomy & Human-Clearance Capacity
The industry-wide lesson is that the bottleneck moved from execution to **human judgment/review capacity**. This RFC governs inflow; this section governs the outflow constraint.

**Automated review is the default first pass.** Line-level review is handled by automated multi-model LLM review (e.g. Greptile + a second-model reviewer via `deft-directive-review-cycle`). That scales and is NOT the human bottleneck.

**The scarce resource is human-clearance throughput** ‚Äî merge approvals (swarm requires a human approval before the first merge), judgment-gate clearances, allocation/Phase-0 approvals, and adjudication when the LLM reviewers disagree or a reviewer errors/escalates.
- Surface a **pending-human-decisions backlog** in `capacity:show` and as a Tier-1 nudge when over threshold.
- `wipCap` SHOULD be tuned relative to clearance throughput, not just machine concurrency.

**Autonomy dial (Observe ‚Üí Escalate ‚Üí Execute).** The gate **tier** decides when automated multi-LLM clearance suffices vs human adjudication is required. An **autonomy level** (per project, optionally per gate-id) ratchets that line: advances as the clearance-override / false-positive rate stays low over a window (earned autonomy), reducing required human clearances; ratchets back on a spike in overrides or rework. Ratchet metric/thresholds ‚Üí Open Question v2.

## Session-Start Nudge Budgeting
Capacity, gates, lifecycle hygiene, review, and cost each generate nudges. The session line is ruthlessly budgeted.

**Headline vs nudge (budget = 1).** `task triage:welcome` shows a state **headline** + **exactly one** primary nudge + a `+N more ‚Üí task capacity:show` overflow. The full ranked list lives only in `capacity:show`.

**Ranking by rate-of-harm-accrual:**
- **Tier 1 (worsening):** stranded slice; gate-blocked-stuck bucket; urgent-bucket overrun; pending-human-decisions backlog over threshold.
- **Tier 2 (trending):** stale undecomposed epic; ungroomed in-flight epic; generic capacity skew; high per-bucket rework rate; cost-skew (a bucket dominating spend).
- **Tier 3 (informational):** advisory-mode (`sample < minSampleSize`); onboarding-incomplete; cache staleness.

Within a tier: magnitude ‚Üí age ‚Üí id. Same-class aggregated. **Tier-1 persists** (escalating wording; provisional); Tier 2/3 suppressed per-window. One shared ranking function backs `triage:welcome` + `capacity:show`.

## Brownfield Backfill (`task capacity:backfill`)
A pre-adoption project (including directive itself) is **classification cold-start**, not volume cold-start: several hundred completed vBRIEFs but none carry `capacityBucket`/`completedAt` and `kind` is partial. `minSampleSize` counts **classified** completions so unclassified history can't falsely activate capacity.

`task capacity:backfill` is a **one-time migration** (same shape as `task migrate:vbrief`):
- `completedAt` ‚Üê git landing time (deterministic, zero human).
- `kind` ‚Üê structural inference (items/references); agent for ambiguous.
- `capacityBucket` ‚Üê match rules ‚Üí agent ‚Üí **human reviews only the low-confidence batch**; records `capacityBucketSource`.
- in-flight epic `estimatedChildren` ‚Üê agent-proposed in the same pass.
- Note: historical **cost** actuals cannot be backfilled (no telemetry exists for past runs) ‚Äî `cost` accrues only forward from adoption, which is why capture must start now.

Human job: confirm the 4 targets + skim the low-confidence batch. **Window-first** activation. **Dry-run-default** + `--apply` (atomic per file, idempotent + resumable, journaled, graceful degradation); **git is the wholesale undo** (+ `--rollback`); **post-backfill validation gate**. **Re-run is a safe no-op** (idempotency + completeness + the completion freeze); re-classification needs `--force --confirm` and never clobbers `human` buckets. **Cross-cutting dry-run convention:** `triage:queue` + `verify:judgment-gates` also offer `--dry-run`/preview.

## Receipts & Audit
Two tiers, reusing existing conventions (no new store):
- **Durable, committed audit** (`vbrief/.audit/*.jsonl`) ‚Äî authority-bearing events: gate clearances, `--force` overrides, allocation approvals, autonomy-level changes. Record-of-record; append-only; must survive.
- **Gitignored telemetry** (`vbrief/.eval/`) ‚Äî high-volume selection-ranking traces. Observability only; capped/rotatable; rebuildable since selection is deterministic.

## Agentic Pre-Grooming + Human Oversight
Agents handle pre-grooming (impact analysis, dedup, risk flagging, bucket/gate proposals, brownfield classification, first-pass review); humans retain final authority on allocation changes, gate clearances, target confirmation, merge approval, and high-context calls. Existing checkpoints (swarm Phase 0, #1378 Gate 0, review-cycle) are reused as the human-approval surface.

## Impact
- **vBRIEF schema:** no changes. New `plan.metadata` keys (`capacityBucket`, `capacityBucketSource`, `rank`, `estimatedChildren`, `weight`, `judgmentGateTags`, `completedAt`, `cost`); gate `tier`/`requiredHumanReviewers`; `plan.policy` arrays (`capacityAllocation`, `judgmentGates`, `judgmentGatesDisabled`) + an `autonomy` policy block.
- **Scripts/Taskfile:** `verify_judgment_gates.py`, `verify_capacity.py`, `capacity_show` (+ outcome/cost columns), `capacity_backfill`, `gate_clear`/`gate_show`; shared nudge-ranking function; deficit-biased selection + spec-readiness eligibility in `triage:queue`; `pathspec` matcher; `scope:promote` epic-aware branch; `scope:complete` stamps `completedAt` + `capacityBucket` + `cost` (when telemetry available); outcome (revert/rework) detector; cost capture via the Warp Analytics API / harness telemetry + a post-hoc `capacity:cost-sync` batch + `deft-directive-cost`.
- **Review/autonomy:** pending-human-decisions surface; autonomy-dial ratchet; gate tiers compose with the review-cycle skill.
- **Skills/renderers:** `deft-directive-triage`, `-refinement`, `-swarm`, `-review-cycle`, `-cost`; `roadmap_render.py` / `project_render.py` respect ordering + buckets.
- **Upstream dependency:** for **enterprise** teams, interactive + cloud cost capture is unblocked via the Warp Enterprise Analytics API (admin-only; requires the Privacy *Enterprise Usage Reporting* toggle). **warpdotdev/warp#12075** is narrowed to the remaining gaps: sub-enterprise (Pro/individual/non-admin) access and raw token/$ exposure (the API reports credits only).
- **Template propagation (#1309):** mirror into `templates/agents-entry.md` + extend `tests/content/test_agents_entry_contract.py`.

## Adoption Experience
- **Greenfield:** setup interview captures targets + active gates + tiers; steady-state `scope:complete` classifies + cost-stamps each item.
- **Brownfield:** run `task capacity:backfill` ‚Äî confirm targets, skim low-confidence batch. Window-first; dry-run-default; git-reversible. Cost accrues forward only.

## Delivery (sliced, not big-bang)
1. **#987 intra-bucket ordering** (`plan.metadata.rank` + renderer / Phase 0) ‚Äî smallest; + spec-readiness eligibility.
2. **Selection ordering** (continuation precedence + deficit-biased selection).
3. **Judgment gate engine** (`judgmentGates[]` + tiers + `verify:judgment-gates` + default-on universals + `gate_clearances[]`/`cleared_scope`).
4. **Capacity accounting** (`capacityAllocation` + kind-aware counting + flow enforcement + `minSampleSize` + `completedAt` + outcome overlay + cost capture/overlay (`capacity:cost-sync` via the Analytics API) + `capacity:show` + shared nudge-ranking + `task capacity:backfill`).
5. **Review & autonomy** (pending-human-decisions surface; autonomy dial; gate-tier composition with review-cycle).
6. **Lifecycle hygiene** (epic staleness + stranded-slice nudge + trichotomy).
7. **Enforcement + #1387 integration** (block modes, headless clearance gating, audit log, block-gated-solo).

## Deferred to v2 (do not lose)
1. **`weighted` capacity unit** (size‚Üíweight). *Stopgap:* `vbrief-count` + `estimatedChildren`.
2. **`cost` capacity unit** (credit/$ denominator). *Stopgap:* cost reporting overlay + `plan.metadata.cost` capture (Analytics API). *Trigger:* enough captured actuals + spend skew that count-mode masks.
3. **Per-commit `vBRIEF:`-trailer attribution for batched block-gated stories.** *Stopgap:* block-gated ship solo.
4. **Secret-content scanner** (key-shaped-string detection). *Stopgap:* path globs + `body-text` + PR-time diff check.
5. **Split `cognitive-debt` into its own bucket.** *Stopgap:* folded into `agentic-debt`. *Trigger:* a **context/doc drift** signal becomes available.
6. **Advisory heuristic annotation for untagged `declared` gates.** *Stopgap:* agent-assisted tagging at grooming.

## Migration & Compatibility
Existing vBRIEF trees remain valid; all new fields default to absent ‚Üí today's behavior. Brownfield projects use `task capacity:backfill`. Cost actuals accrue forward only.

## Decisions Log
- **OQ1 ‚Äî default `match` globs:** "both, layered."
- **OQ2 ‚Äî `weighted` capacity:** deferred to v2 (`vbrief-count` + `estimatedChildren`).
- **OQ3 ‚Äî `declared`-gate PR-time check:** honor tier for detectable/tagged; annotate-only for untagged; optional `paths` backstop.
- **OQ4 ‚Äî `defaultEpicEstimate` + staleness:** default 3; absent ‚Üí immediate nudge; `epicStalenessDays` default 14.
- **Clearance staleness:** binds to `cleared_scope`; scope creep re-triggers.
- **Re-bucketing:** in-flight allowed, frozen at completion.
- **Gate-blocked = reserved capacity:** counts toward bucket; `blocked` sub-count surfaced.
- **Multi-vBRIEF PR attribution:** `vBRIEF:` trailer + `file_scope` cross-check + fail-closed, pre-squash; v1 block-gated-solo.
- **Small-N balancing:** enforce over the completion flow; deficit-biased selection; `minSampleSize` guard. `wipCap` = concurrency, buckets = longitudinal mix.
- **Running totals:** derived; no stored total; optional rebuildable cache.
- **Effort vs value:** capacity measures effort; separate **outcome overlay** (revert/rework) and **cost overlay** close the loop.
- **Stranded slices:** completed slice keeps its bucket; debt forward-recognized via `epicStrandedDays` + trichotomy.
- **Continuation precedence:** `urgent/blocking > continuation > net-new(deficit) > rank > date`; oldest-started first; cancel valve; optional `finishBeforeStart`.
- **Session-start nudge budgeting:** budget 1 + overflow; rate-of-harm tiers; aggregated; Tier-1 persists; shared ranking function.
- **Brownfield cold-start:** `minSampleSize` counts *classified* completions; `task capacity:backfill` one-time migration; window-first; dry-run-default; re-run safe no-op; `--force --confirm` never clobbers `human`.
- **Review model:** automated multi-LLM review is the default first pass; the scarce resource is **human-clearance throughput**, surfaced as a pending-decisions backlog and relieved by the autonomy dial.
- **Risk-tiered gates:** gates carry a `tier` (`auto`/`review`/`block`) + `requiredHumanReviewers`, not binary; composes with the autonomy dial.
- **Spec-readiness eligibility:** only swarm-ready/well-specified work is promotable; reuse `swarm:readiness`.
- **Cost capture (rev 12 ‚Üí rev 13):** start collecting actuals now via additive `plan.metadata.cost = { credits, tokens, usd, source, partial, asOf }` ‚Äî **never agent self-report**. Primary source is the **Warp Enterprise Analytics API** `events` endpoint (per-message `credit_charged`, covering interactive + cloud), synced post-hoc by **`task capacity:cost-sync`** and attributed via `git_context.branch`/`head` + `conversation_id` (with the `vBRIEF:` trailer for batched sessions); harness OTEL / API `usage` fill `tokens` where available; `usd` derived via a credit‚Üí$ rate; `source`+`partial` provenance. Two-tier coverage: **enterprise-grounded** vs **estimate-only** (`deft-directive-cost`) below enterprise. **warpdotdev/warp#12075** narrowed to sub-enterprise access + raw token/$; cost-as-`unit` deferred.
- **Cognitive-debt drift:** context/doc drift is the measurable signal that could graduate cognitive debt to its own bucket.
- **Receipts:** durable committed audit (`vbrief/.audit/`) for authority-bearing events; gitignored `vbrief/.eval/` telemetry for selection traces.

## Open Questions v2 (research-informed)
- **Autonomy ratchet metric:** exact signal + thresholds for advancing/retreating the autonomy level.
- **Cost as a capacity `unit`** vs a permanent reporting overlay (depends on captured-actuals coverage; now grounded for enterprise via the Analytics API ‚Äî credits, not tokens; sub-enterprise coverage pending warpdotdev/warp#12075).
- **Cognitive-debt graduation:** which drift metric is reliable enough to make it its own bucket.
- **LLM-reviewer disagreement routing:** policy for when conflicting/erroring multi-LLM reviews escalate to human adjudication (and how that interacts with gate tiers).

## References
- #987 (originating issue)
- `scripts/triage_classify.py` (match DSL + universal-rule layering)
- `scripts/policy.py`, `task policy:show` (typed-policy + audit-row pattern)
- `scripts/swarm_readiness.py` (`file_scope`/spec-readiness contract)
- `task migrate:vbrief` (one-time migration pattern reused by `capacity:backfill`)
- `deft-directive-review-cycle` (multi-LLM review), `deft-directive-cost` (pre-build estimate; calibrated by post-build actuals)
- Warp Enterprise Analytics API ‚Äî https://docs.warp.dev/enterprise/enterprise-features/analytics-api/ (`summary`/`users`/`events`; per-message `credit_charged` + `git_context`; enterprise/admin-only; primary cost-capture source)
- Upstream: **warpdotdev/warp#12075** (narrowed to sub-enterprise access + raw token/$ exposure; enterprise interactive+cloud capture now unblocked via the Analytics API)
- AGENTS.md: pre-`start_agent` gate stack (#1378), headless launch (#1387), template propagation (#1309), SCM boundary (#1145), filesystem-truth in-flight count (#1270), swarm per-story checkpoint commits, session-start ritual + D2 suppression (#1149 / #1143)
- `vbrief/vbrief.md` (kind / epic-story model, decomposition)
- Industry best-practice basis: Anthropic 2026 Agentic Coding Trends Report; 2025 DORA findings; CodeScene (code-health/coverage signals); autonomy-dial + risk-tiered-escalation patterns; multi-agent production post-mortems (cost caps, cascade/blast-radius, receipts-not-chat-logs).

references.title: Issue #1419: RFC: Agentic Prioritization & Coordination Model for Deft Directive

Build the risk-tiered judgment-gate engine and verify:judgment-gates
Add capacity allocation accounting with buckets, windows, and capacity:show
Surface human-clearance backlog and the earned-autonomy dial
Add epic-staleness and stranded-slice lifecycle nudges with trichotomy
Integrate gate clearances and capacity blocks into Gate 0 and swarm:launch
